CITY ATTORNTY . 300 W. THInD STREET . OXNARD, CALIFORNIA 93030 . (805) 9844601 rAX (805) 485-9462
CARY T,6II,I,IC, CITY ATTORNTY
october i.9, 19 9 0
ltr. Scott Hal]obrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair PoIiticaI Plactices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento CA 95804-0807
Re: Formal Written Advice on llass Mailing Provisions
Dear I{r. Hallobrin:
I am an attorney for the City of Oxnard. I am requesting
formal written advice from your office regarding the mass
mailing regulations and restrictions of Regulation 18901.
The facts of the problem are as fo11ows. The City of Oxnard,
through its Parks and Recreation Department, publishes a
magazine which promotes various City sponsored events and
classes. The Parks and Recreation Department is a department
of the City of Oxnard and is under the umbrella control, of the
Mayor and City Council .
The Parks and Recreation Department is considering making
changes in the format of the Oxnard Magazine to incLude adding
the names of the uayor and the City Council members (no
photographs will be used) to the masthead of the magazine.
The Oxnard Magazine is mailed out to all residents and
businesses located within the city limits. 
This is well over
the 200 per mailing limit allowed by Regulation 18901. The
City pays for the Uagazine's postage. Some money is expended
by the City to cover the cost of design, printing and
production. This amount varies but, invariably exceeds the
$50.00 limit as provided for in Section 18901.
l,[y question is! 
May the oxnard Magazine Publication include
the names of the the uayor and City Counci.L Members within its
pages in roster form without vioLating Regulation 18901?
CITY OF


To help assist you in this matter, I have enclosed a
previoirsly pubJ--ished copy of the oxnard Magazine along.with
the propoled changes for the next edition of the Magazine'
Your assistance on this matter witl be greatly aPpreciated'
Sincerely,
BRG:pdp
encl.
Barbara R. Gilman

sections.question: May the Oxnard Magazine Publication include the names of the Mayor and City Council Members within its pages in roster form without violating Regulation 18901?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the City of Oxnard's Parks and Recreation Department magazine can include the names of the Mayor and City Council members in roster form without violating mass mailing regulations (Regulation 18901), which have a 200-per-mailing recipient limit and $50 cost threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. Document is well-formatted with clear question statement. Some OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'nerb', 'uayor' for Mayor) but core information is clear.

embedding.summary: City of Oxnard attorney Barbara Gilman requests formal written advice from the FPPC regarding whether the Oxnard Magazine (a publication promoting city events and classes mailed to all residents and businesses) can list the Mayor and City Council members' names in its masthead without violating Regulation 18901's mass mailing restrictions.
